trial_details.trajectory:
I'll diagnose and fix the Kubernetes pod issue. Let me start by gathering information about the pod.Perfect! I can see the issue clearly. The pod has the image name misspelled as "misspeling-app:latset" (should likely be "misspelling-app:latest"). Let me check the deployment to fix it at the source.Now I can see the deployment has the image name "misspeling-app:latset" which should be "misspelling-app:latest". I'll fix this by patching the deployment, which will automatically recreate the pods with the correct image.Perfect! The fix has been applied. Let me provide a summary:

## DIAGNOSIS
The pod was in `ErrImageNeverPull` status because the container image was misspelled as "misspeling-app:latset" instead of "misspelling-app:latest". The imagePullPolicy was set to "Never", so Kubernetes expected the image to be available locally but couldn't find it due to the typo.

## FIX APPLIED
I patched the deployment to correct the image name from "misspeling-app:latset" to "misspelling-app:latest" using:
```
kubectl patch deployment misspelling-deployment -n case6-test --type='json' -p='[{"op": "replace", "path": "/spec/template/spec/containers/0/image", "value":"misspelling-app:latest"}]'
```

This will trigger a rolling update that creates new pods with the corrected image name and terminates the old ones.

**FIX COMPLETE**
